creator: @quebert
executive_summary: With CPI at a 22-month high and Iran geopolitical risk unresolved, the moderate portfolio anchors on gold as the highest conviction, lowest risk position while adding AI infrastructure (NVDA) and an oversold large-cap recovery (MSFT) for growth exposure. A 15% cash reserve preserves tactical dry powder for post-earnings re-entries on TSM (April 16) and GOOGL/MSFT (April 29), balancing the greed-elevated market environment with disciplined stop-loss and DCA execution on every binary-catalyst position.

macro_environment.summary: Mixed macro regime: positive yield curve (+0.724%) and F&G 65.4 (Greed) support risk assets, but CPI at 3.3% (22-month high) and Iran-Islamabad ceasefire breakdown introduce stagflation and geopolitical risk. SPY RSI 67.8 approaching overbought warrants selectivity over broad index exposure. Simultaneous gold and BTC strength confirms hard asset / fiat-debasement thesis as dominant macro narrative.
key_factors: CPI 3.3% (22-month high) sustains hard asset thesis — gold and BTC as inflation and fiat-debasement hedges simultaneously
Positive yield curve +0.724% reduces recession probability and supports selective equity exposure in AI infrastructure
Iran-Islamabad talks collapsed — Hormuz risk intact, gold geopolitical premium maintained despite oil -10.5% weekly pullback
AI capex cycle accelerating — NVDA forward PE 16.97 signals structural underpricing vs +73.2% YoY revenue growth trajectory

cross_sector_insights.insight: Gold and BTC both bullish simultaneously — hard assets bid across the board
cross_sector_insights.implication: Fiat devaluation hedge is the dominant macro theme. Overweight materials (gold) and add crypto allocation despite volatility penalty. Both assets benefit from CPI 3.3% and BRICS+ dedollarization narrative. 20% materials + 10% crypto = 30% hard asset allocation justified for moderate profile.

cross_sector_insights.insight: AI hardware (NVDA +70% YTD, TSM +26.5%) diverging sharply from AI software (MSFT -21.6%) — sector rotation in progress
cross_sector_insights.implication: Favor AI infrastructure plays (NVDA, GOOGL) over pure software. MSFT's capitulatory RSI 39.5 represents a mean-reversion opportunity, not a structural short — Copilot Code Red catalyst and April 29 earnings are reload triggers, not sell signals.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Oil -10.5% weekly (Iran ceasefire optimism) vs Gold unchanged/up — geopolitical premium migrating from commodity to safe haven
cross_sector_insights.implication: No new oil longs at $96.57 after the weekly spike down. Gold captures geopolitical risk premium more efficiently here. XAU -8.3% 30d pullback from $5,178 has reset technicals; 3 consecutive weekly gains confirm stabilization.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Earnings cluster risk: KMI (Apr 15), TSM (Apr 16), MSFT and GOOGL (both Apr 29)
cross_sector_insights.implication: For moderate profile, maintain 15% cash buffer for post-earnings re-entry windows. Binary catalysts in <5 days on KMI and TSM create high-reward setups — DCA sizing on MSFT and GOOGL mitigates concurrent April 29 event risk.

Iran-Islamabad talks collapsed — Hormuz risk persists, geopolitical premium intact. Stop at $4,450 below consolidation floor. Target $5,800 extrapolates current macro trend acceleration.

Key support $182.07 provides tight stop at $181.00.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Bitcoin
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Strong macro tailwinds: $240M single-day ETF inflows, MicroStrategy 766,970 BTC, BTC dominance 57.3% cycle-high, recovering from Feb extreme-fear lows. CPI 3.3% reinforces dual gold+BTC inflation hedge thesis. Key risk: triple rejection at $73K resistance. DCA in 3 tranches: $72,825 (40%), ~$68,000 (30%), ~$63,500 (30%). Hard stop below $63,000.

historical_accuracy.notable: Market closed Sunday April 12 — all 6 April 11 positions flat vs Friday close: TSM@$370.60 (0%), NVDA@$188.63 (0%), XAU@$4,751.68 (-0.1%), AMD@$245.04 (0%), AMZN@$238.38 (0%), SI@$75.68 (+0.1%). Accuracy tracking starts Monday April 13. Key rotation: AMD and AMZN exited top-6 (real RSI confirmed overbought: 73.7 and 75.3 respectively); MSFT and GOOGL entered as data-driven additions.

warnings: TSM earnings April 16 (4 days) — binary event with RSI 68.7 at fair entry. Moderate investors may prefer post-earnings entry to avoid overnight gap risk; EV/EBITDA 2.93 makes a post-beat dip very buyable.
SPY RSI 67.8 approaching overbought — avoid chasing high-RSI names: AMD (73.7), BAC (79.0), AMZN (75.3), NFLX (80.3) all excluded from portfolio on real overbought signals from pre_fetch data.
BTC triple rejection at $73K resistance — three failed breakout attempts form a concerning technical pattern; DCA entry and strict stop at $63,000 are non-negotiable for moderate profile.
MSFT and GOOGL both reporting April 29 — concurrent mega-cap earnings amplify event risk; DCA sizing on both directly mitigates this correlation.
Iran-Islamabad talks collapsed — renewed Hormuz escalation could spike VIX above 25 and trigger broad risk-off; XAU position and 15% cash buffer serve as dual portfolio hedge.

crypto.sector_summary: Crypto recovering from Feb 2026 extreme-fear lows (F&G bottomed at 5). Total market cap ~$2.54T, BTC dominance 57.3% cycle-high. Bitcoin leading recovery with strong institutional ETF inflows ($240M single session). ETH showing solid 30d momentum (+10.1%). SOL impacted by $275M Drift Protocol exploit.
crypto.top_pick_reasoning: Bitcoin combines the strongest institutional demand signals (record ETF inflows, MicroStrategy accumulation), most favorable macro narrative (CPI 3.3% digital gold thesis), and cycle-high dominance (57.3%) showing capital prioritizes BTC over altcoins.

AMD/AMZN/BAC rejected on real-data RSI overbought signals (73-79).

materials.sector_summary: Precious metals dominated: XAU ($4,747) posted 3rd consecutive weekly gain (+2%), supported by collapsed US-Iran talks, CPI 3.3% (22-month high), and accelerating central bank purchases. Silver ($75.76) outperformed on weekly basis (+4.2%) benefiting from safe-haven flows and structural solar sector demand (YTD +134.6%). Crude oil WTI ($96.57) suffered the largest weekly decline in 9 months (-10.5%) on ceasefire optimism, but Islamabad talks breakdown reintroduces sharp upside risk Monday.
materials.top_pick_reasoning: Gold offers strongest risk-adjusted return: accelerating central bank purchases, US inflation at 22-month highs, rising Fed rate-cut expectations weakening dollar, total breakdown of US-Iran negotiations, BRICS+ dedollarization — all converging simultaneously.
